question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an environment variable HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_PARSER_BALANCE_BATCHSIZE=2147483647 to trigger integer overflow in `batchSize * 5L` during constructor initialization, causing `batch.setMaxItems()` to compute a negative or unexpected maxItems value, leading to fee-bearing balance records being silently dropped or processed outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted Spring Boot configuration property `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.transactionTimeout=1s` (bypassing the design default of 5 minutes) via an externally accessible config endpoint or environment variable, causing the DB transaction processing fee-related balance data to time out and roll back, resulting in fee modifications being lost outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBatchSize()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.batchSize=1` (the minimum allowed by `@Min(1)`) to force the balance parser to flush fee-bearing account balance records one at a time, causing excessive partial commits that allow fee data to be persisted in an inconsistent intermediate state outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFileBufferSize()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.fileBufferSize` to an arbitrarily large integer (no upper bound enforced by `@Min(1)`) to exhaust JVM heap memory during balance file reading, causing the importer to crash mid-parse and leave fee-related balance records in a partially committed state that deviates from design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.retry.maxAttempts=0` to override the constructor-set default of 3, causing the balance parser to never retry failed fee-related balance file parsing, resulting in fee data being permanently skipped and balances reflecting incorrect post-fee states?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.retry.maxAttempts=2147483647` (Integer.MAX_VALUE, allowed by `@Min(0)` in RetryProperties) to cause the balance parser to retry indefinitely on a crafted malformed balance file, blocking all subsequent fee-related balance processing and causing a denial-of-service on fee data ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.frequency=10ms` (the minimum allowed by `@DurationMin(millis=10L)`) to cause the balance parser to poll for new balance files at 100x the design rate of 1 second, creating a resource exhaustion condition that corrupts the ordering of fee-related balance snapshots processed outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.enabled=false` via an externally reachable configuration surface to disable the entire balance parser, causing all fee-deducted account balance snapshots to stop being ingested, making the mirror node report stale pre-fee balances to downstream consumers outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.batch.queueCapacity` to an extremely large value (no upper bound in `BatchProperties`) to cause unbounded memory allocation in the balance file queue, leading to OOM and loss of fee-related balance data mid-processing outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.batch.maxItems=1` (minimum allowed by `@Min(1)`) to override the constructor-computed value of `batchSize * 5L`, forcing the batch processor to flush after every single balance record, causing fee-related balance data to be committed in micro-batches that violate the atomicity guarantees of the design?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.batch.flushInterval=100ms` (the minimum allowed by `@DurationMin(millis=100L)`) to force the batch flusher to commit fee-related balance data 20x more frequently than the design default of 2 seconds, creating race conditions in fee data persistence outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.batch.window=100ms` (the minimum allowed by `@DurationMin(millis=100L)`) to shrink the batching window from the design default of 5 minutes, causing the importer to prematurely exit batch mode during high-volume fee processing and process balance files individually at a rate that corrupts fee data ordering?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.retry.minBackoff=100ms` (the minimum allowed by `@DurationMin(millis=100L)`) to reduce the retry delay from the design default, causing the balance parser to hammer the database with rapid retries on fee-related balance file failures, amplifying the impact of any transient fee data corruption?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.retry.maxBackoff=500ms` (the minimum allowed by `@DurationMin(millis=500L)`) to cap the maximum retry backoff far below the design default of 10 seconds, causing the balance parser to retry fee-related balance file failures at a high rate that overwhelms the database and prevents correct fee data from being committed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.retry.multiplier=1` (the minimum allowed by `@Min(1)`) to disable exponential backoff growth, causing the balance parser to retry fee-related balance file failures at a constant minimum interval rather than backing off, leading to sustained database pressure that disrupts fee data ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.processingTimeout=5s` (the minimum allowed by `@DurationMin(seconds=5)`) to reduce the processing timeout from the design default of 10 seconds, causing the health check to falsely report the balance parser as unhealthy during legitimate fee-related balance file processing, triggering unnecessary restarts that lose in-flight fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.batch.maxFiles` to an extremely large integer (no upper bound in `BatchProperties`) to cause the balance parser to batch an unbounded number of fee-related balance files into a single transaction, causing the `transactionTimeout` of 5 minutes to be exceeded and all fee data in the batch to be rolled back?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getStreamType()` always returns `StreamType.BALANCE` with no runtime guard to craft a scenario where misconfigured `batchSize` or `transactionTimeout` causes the balance stream type's fee-related data to be processed under record-stream parameters, leading to fee data being stored with incorrect timestamps or amounts outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.transactionTimeout` to an extremely large value (no upper bound enforced beyond `@DurationMin(seconds=1)`) to cause the balance parser to hold database locks for an extended period during fee-related balance processing, blocking concurrent fee schedule updates from being committed and effectively modifying the observable fee state outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.batchSize=2147483647` to cause the expression `batchSize * 5L` in the constructor to produce a `maxItems` value of 10737418235L, which is far beyond the design intent of 1,000,000, causing the batch processor to accumulate an unbounded number of fee-related balance records before flushing, leading to memory exhaustion and loss of fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.batch.queueCapacity=1` (already the constructor default) combined with `batchSize=1` to create a scenario where the single-slot queue is perpetually full during fee-related balance file processing, causing the producer thread to block indefinitely and starve fee data ingestion outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a `@Max` constraint on `fileBufferSize` to set it to `Integer.MAX_VALUE` (2,147,483,647 bytes), causing the balance file reader to attempt to allocate a ~2GB buffer per file, triggering OOM during fee-related balance file parsing and causing the importer to crash before fee data is committed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.frequency` to a very large duration value (no upper bound enforced by `@DurationMin(millis=10L)`) to cause the balance parser to poll for new fee-related balance files only once every several hours, making the mirror node serve stale fee-deducted balance data to downstream consumers for extended periods outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/balance/BalanceParserProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BalanceParserProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.parser.balance.retry.maxAttempts=1` to reduce retries below the constructor default of 3, causing a single transient failure during fee-related balance file parsing to permanently skip that file, resulting in fee deductions from that period being absent from the mirror node's balance data outside design parameters?
